Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Failure of the Thermodynamics of Computation(2010) 30 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. A Verilog to Factorio Compiler and Simulator (Working RISC-V CPU) 127 upvotes, 15 comments
  3. OpenBSD on Motorola 88000 Processors 134 upvotes, 18 comments
  4. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 1254 upvotes, 242 comments
  5. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 432 upvotes, 76 comments
  6. App that shows real-time lightning on Earth is showing bombings in Middle East 17 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. CSS is DOOMed 436 upvotes, 103 comments
  8. Say No to Palantir in Europe 252 upvotes, 58 comments
  9. Show HN: Create a full language server in Go with 3.17 spec support 47 upvotes, 9 comments
  10. I decompiled the White House's new app 589 upvotes, 215 comments
  11. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 121 upvotes, 45 comments
  12. Overestimation of microplastics potentially caused by scientists' gloves 354 upvotes, 154 comments
  13. Technology: The (nearly) perfect USB cable tester does exist 187 upvotes, 83 comments
  14. Full network of clitoral nerves mapped out for first time 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Show HN: Public transit systems as data – lines, stations, railcars, and history 36 upvotes, 12 comments
  16. The Epistemology of Microphysics 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND 90 upvotes, 41 comments
  18. Siclair Microvision (1977) 40 upvotes, 15 comments
  19. What if AI doesn't need more RAM but better math? 130 upvotes, 73 comments
  20. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 240 upvotes, 159 comments
  21. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 186 upvotes, 123 comments
  22. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 250 upvotes, 173 comments
  23. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 178 upvotes, 125 comments
  24. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 724 upvotes, 574 comments
  25. Figma's MCP Update Reflects a Larger Industry Shift 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  26. Nonfiction Publishing, Under Threat, Is More Important 36 upvotes, 23 comments
  27. Stop Publishing Garbage Data, It's Embarrassing 14 upvotes, 6 comments
  28. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Building a Mostly IPv6 Only Home Network 45 upvotes, 54 comments
  30. Comparison shows audiophiles waste a lot of money 7 upvotes, 6 comments

Last generated 12:30am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
